sections.paragraph:
-

(1) to be uncircumcised, see the adj. עָרֵל . Arab. غَرِلَ id.

(2) denom. from עָרְלָה, to regard as uncircumcised, i.e. profane, impure, Lev 19:23.

Niphal, to be seen to be uncircumcised, Hab 2:16 (used of a drunken man who shamefully uncovers his nakedness).

ערל

m. const. עֶרֶל Eze 44:9, and עֲרַל Exo 6:12, adj. uncircumcised, Gen 17:14 Exo 12:48 often used opprobriously of the Gentiles, as the Philistines, 1Sa 17:26, 36 1Sa 17:36 14:6 31:4 . Metaph. used עֲרַל שְׂפָתַיִם uncircumcised of lips, i.e. slow of speech (יַקִּיר מַמְלַל Onk.), stammering, one whose lips are closed as it were with the foreskin, and are therefore too long and thick to utter speech with facility. Exo 6:12, 30 . Similarly Jer 6:10, עֲרֵלָה אָזְנָם “their ear is closed with a foreskin;” and לְבָבָם חֶעָרֵל their uncircumcised heart, into which divine precepts cannot penetrate, Lev 26:41 Eze 44:9.
